questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut is used to add a comment to specific text in Notion?

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M opens the comment menu for the selected text.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + C
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + C is the standard shortcut for copying text in most applications.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + B
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + B is not a standard Notion shortcut for comments.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + /
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + / opens the formatting menu for the current block, not a comment.

questions.question: How do you create a bulleted list using Markdown shortcuts?

options.text: Type # followed by a space
options.explanation: Type # followed by a space creates an H1 header.

options.text: Type * or - or + followed by a space
options.explanation: Typing *, -, or + followed by a space creates a bulleted list.

options.text: Type [] followed by a space
options.explanation: Type [] followed by a space creates a to-do checkbox.

options.text: Type 1. followed by a space
options.explanation: Type 1. followed by a space creates a numbered list.

questions.question: Which three typography styles can you choose from for your page?

options.text: Sans-serif, Serif, and Handwritten
options.explanation: Notion offers Default, Serif, and Mono typography styles.

options.text: Default, Serif, and Mono
options.explanation: You can select Default, Serif, or Mono styles via the menu at the top right.

options.text: Clean, Modern, and Classic
options.explanation: These are not the specific typography style options available in Notion.

options.text: Arial, Helvetica, and Times New Roman
options.explanation: Notion uses its own system fonts rather than specific system names.

questions.question: What is the quickest way to reuse the last color you applied to text?

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + C
options.explanation: There is no standard shortcut for copying colors.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + H
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + H applies the last color you used.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + V
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + V pastes text, it does not apply formatting.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + Color
options.explanation: You must type the specific color name or use the shortcut H to recall the last one.

questions.question: How can you add a callout block to a page?

options.text: Hover over a new block and select + Callout
options.explanation: You can select Callout from the + menu on a new block.

options.text: Type /callout at the top of the page
options.explanation: Typing /callout and pressing enter creates a callout block.

options.text: Type @callout
options.explanation: The command for callouts is /callout, not @callout.

options.text: Click the Callout icon in the sidebar
options.explanation: There is no Callout icon in the sidebar to insert blocks.

questions.question: How do you remove the icon from a callout block?

options.text: Click the icon and select Delete
options.explanation: You select 'Remove' from the icon menu to delete it.

options.text: Click the icon and select Remove
options.explanation: Clicking the icon allows you to choose 'Remove' to delete it.

options.text: Right-click the callout and choose Remove icon
options.explanation: You cannot right-click to remove the icon; you must click it.

options.text: The icon cannot be removed once added
options.explanation: You can easily remove the icon by clicking it and selecting Remove.

questions.question: How do you change the background color of a callout block?

options.text: Highlight the text and select Color
options.explanation: That method changes the text color. To change the background, click the `⋮⋮` icon.

options.text: Click the `⋮⋮` icon, select Color, and choose a background color
options.explanation: The `⋮⋮` icon allows you to select 'Color' to change the block's background.

options.text: Type /background and select a color
options.explanation: There is no /background command; use the `⋮⋮` menu instead.

options.text: Select the block and press Cmd/Ctrl + B
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + B bolds the text inside the block, not the background.

questions.question: How do you add cover art to a page?

options.text: Click the image icon in the top left corner
options.explanation: The top left icon is for the page icon, not cover art.

options.text: Hover over the top of the page and click Add cover
options.explanation: Hovering over the top of the page reveals the 'Add cover' button.

options.text: Go to the ••• menu and select Add cover
options.explanation: Cover art is added by hovering over the top edge of the page, not the menu.

options.text: Select 'Cover' from the formatting menu
options.explanation: Cover art is a page-level setting, not a block formatting option.

questions.question: How do you add an inline emoji using text?

options.text: Type : followed by the emoji name (e.g., :fire)
options.explanation: Type : and the name (like :fire) to render the emoji inline.

options.text: Type @ followed by the emoji name
options.explanation: The @ symbol is used for mentions, not inline emojis.

options.text: Type # followed by the emoji name
options.explanation: # creates a header, not an emoji.

options.text: Type /emoji and select it from the list
options.explanation: /emoji opens the emoji menu, but typing :name is the shortcut for inline.

questions.question: How do you toggle the 'Show backlinks' setting for a database page?

options.text: Click Customize page and toggle Show backlinks
options.explanation: Customize page is for non-database pages. Database pages require Customize layout.

options.text: Click Customize layout, select Heading module, and toggle Show backlinks
options.explanation: For databases, you must go to Customize layout, click the Heading module, and toggle the setting.

options.text: Click the settings button next to the database name
options.explanation: That button controls comment styles, not backlinks visibility.

options.text: Hover over the top right of the page and select Customize
options.explanation: The top right menu is for page settings, not database layout customization.

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum width for cover images?

options.text: 500 pixels wide
options.explanation: Notion recommends images at least 1,500 pixels wide for best results.

options.text: 1,500 pixels wide
options.explanation: Using images at least 1,500 pixels wide ensures they look good on various screens.

options.text: 1,024 pixels wide
options.explanation: 1,024 is the standard width for many web elements, but Notion recommends 1,500 for covers.

options.text: Any size is fine, aspect ratio doesn't matter
options.explanation: Notion images are dynamic, but 1,500px is the recommended minimum width.

questions.question: Which shortcut allows you to display inline code in your text?

options.text: Cmd/ctrl + E
options.explanation: Cmd/ctrl + E displays selected text as inline code.

options.text: Cmd/ctrl + S
options.explanation: Cmd/ctrl + S is used for strikethrough.

options.text: Cmd/ctrl + I
options.explanation: Cmd/ctrl + I is used for italics.

options.text: Cmd/ctrl + K
options.explanation: Cmd/ctrl + K is not a standard text styling shortcut in Notion.

questions.question: How do you enable 'Full width' mode for a page?

options.text: Click the ••• menu and toggle on Full width
options.explanation: The ••• menu in the top right allows you to toggle Full width on.

options.text: Go to Page settings and select Full width
options.explanation: The toggle is located in the ••• menu, not a dedicated Page settings tab for this specific feature.

options.text: Type /fullwidth
options.explanation: There is no /fullwidth command; it is a menu toggle.

options.text: Full width can only be applied to database pages
options.explanation: Full width can be toggled on for any non-database page.

questions.question: When customizing backlinks and comments for a non-database page, where do you find the 'Show backlinks' toggle?

options.text: In the Customize page window
options.explanation: For non-database pages, you click Customize page to access the window with these toggles.

options.text: In the Customize layout window
options.explanation: Customize layout is specifically for database pages.

options.text: In the settings menu next to the database name
options.explanation: That menu is only available for database pages.

options.text: It is not possible to toggle backlinks on non-database pages
options.explanation: You can toggle backlinks and page discussions for non-database pages via Customize page.

questions.question: What happens if you select a non-black color for the text inside a callout block?

options.text: The block turns white with a light gray outline
options.explanation: Selecting a non-black color for text automatically changes the block to a white background with a light gray outline.

options.text: The block remains transparent
options.explanation: The block always has a visible background when text color is changed.

options.text: The block turns black
options.explanation: The block background remains white; only the text changes color.

options.text: The block turns red
options.explanation: The block background stays white; the color change applies to the text.
